United Premier Soccer League · New Orleans Metro Area
The City League
UPSL Premier Framework New Orleans, LA Community-Anchored
The Pipeline
New Orleans has a population, culture, and history more akin to a European city — but lacks the infrastructure, policy, and geographic proximity to build and connect its talent to the wider system of US Soccer. The City League changes that, sitting atop a longstanding local player pipeline that is open and available for all. Players of all ages and stages have used City Soccer as their developmental base. The City League provides the platform to stabilize a Premier-level league in the UPSL framework.
Premier League
National Competition · Play-In Eligible
The Goal the Entire System Builds Toward
Premier League is operated by UPSL — separate from The City League. Teams that earn their way through the City League pipeline become eligible for UPSL Play-In competition and full Premier League membership.
The City League
1
D1 / A Division · 8–10 Teams
The City League
U23/U20 Led · Pro Teams Welcome
Competitive Adult Play
8–10 teams competing at D1/A Division level. The majority are U23/U20 player-led sides with assigned coaches and elite adult mentors. Professional teams seeking a pathway to the UPSL Premier League are also welcome — making this the most competitive environment in the City Soccer ecosystem.
2
Transition to Adult
The Varsity League
Transitional · Accessible · Compatible
Ensuring Preparedness
Built for the moment most players fall through the cracks. The Varsity League holds U18/19 players in a competitive, structured environment during the hardest transition in American soccer — from youth to adult. No one gets left behind at the door.
City Soccer Programs
3
Competitive Development
Open Academy
Ready · Compete · Perform
Complete Principle Understanding
Most American players can execute. Few can read the game. Open Academy closes that gap — teaching principles-of-play that work with any system, any coach, and any team. The result is a player who thinks the game, not just plays it.
4
Extending Foundations
Age-Based Programming
10–13 · 13–16 · 16–19
Continued Fun & Prep
Age-grouped sessions designed to support players building toward Open Academy readiness. Each group works on core competencies tied to the City Soccer curriculum — giving players the reps, confidence, and tactical foundation they need before stepping into competitive league programming.
5
Building a Love for the Game
City Soccer Prep
Touch · Move · Love the Game
Fun Foundations
The foundation of everything. City Soccer Prep meets players where they are and builds the skills, habits, and love for the game that every level above depends on.

THE CITY LEAGUE
The City League
City League Tier Structure
Tier Teams Description & Role in the Ecosystem Level
Premier League
 4–6 Teams City League-operated under the UPSL framework. Premier players and teams emerge from within the system — proven, vetted, and ready. Consistent performance at the New Orleans hub is the only path to Premier League play. UPSL Framework
The City League
 6–8 Teams 8–10 teams at D1/A Division level. Majority U23/U20 player-led sides with assigned coaches and elite adult mentors. Professional teams seeking a UPSL Premier pathway are welcome. Game film sessions at The City Hub keep players developing between matches. D1 / A Division
The Varsity League
 6–8 Teams Built for the U18/19 transition — holding players in a competitive, structured environment during the hardest gap in American soccer. Bridges youth to adult registration while keeping the Open Academy learning environment intact. U18/19 Transition
New Orleans Context
Built on realistic local conditions. The New Orleans Jesters (NPSL) serve as a comparable benchmark for Premier-level play — a concrete reference point for what competing at this level actually requires.
Sufficient metro talent exists to field four genuine Premier-level teams without a full travel-team model.
Travel costs are prohibitive for elite adults given regional geography — local-first is the sustainable path.
U20/U23-driven rosters make Premier play financially viable while generating family investment.
Elite adults exceeding local levels are guided toward full professional opportunities — keeping the Incubator focused on player growth.
Thorough registration and vetting minimize mid-season dropout and protect competitive integrity at every tier.
INFRASTRUCTURE
Infrastructure
🏟️
Game Day Stadium
Official UPSL match days hosted at an established venue such as Pan American Stadium — delivering a professional experience for players, families, and the broader New Orleans soccer community. Reinforces legitimacy and gives Incubator players the authentic competitive atmosphere their growth demands.
⚽
Soccer Specific Facility
A purpose-built turf practice field (min. 100×65 yards) with technical training areas forms the daily operational backbone. Secured through a 10+ year metro-area lease, enabling the consistent high-frequency training environment that genuine player growth demands.
🎯
The City Hub
Centralized at the practice facility, The City Hub is the cultural and operational core of City Soccer. It hosts game film review, strategic sessions, social events, and community programming — building cohesion off the field and reinforcing this is more than a league. It's a player growth community.
Regional Expansion Blueprint
The City Soccer model is deliberately architected for replication. Two to three comparable central sites — each anchored by their own hub facility — can replicate the full player growth ecosystem, with New Orleans as the proven blueprint.
🗺️
City · State · Regional Scaling

Defined expansion criteria at each level ensure new markets join with appropriate infrastructure, competitive readiness, and community ties in place — not simply as added teams filling a schedule.

🤝
Shared Programming Model

The community programming, vetting, and registration framework built in New Orleans is shared with expansion partners — maintaining consistency in quality and identity across all sites.

📅
Year-Round Programming Calendar

Challenge Cup tournaments, visiting summer teams, College Prep events, and Regional Premier club exposure sessions create a 12-month program — a full growth environment, not just a season.
